subject: Cloud Computing
title: What Are Microservices?
description: A 60-second lesson on What Are Microservices?.

hook.text: Netflix runs over 1,000 microservices. Each one does one thing, and they communicate over the network.

buildup.text: Microservices split an application into small, independent services. Each has its own database and deployment cycle.

discovery.text: Teams can build, test, and deploy each service independently. No waiting for other teams to finish.

twist.text: Microservices solve organization problems, not technical ones. Small teams don't need 50 services.

climax.text: The overhead is real: service discovery, distributed tracing, network failures. Simple systems become complex.

punchline.text: Microservices scale teams. But they multiply complexity.

quiz.question: What problem do microservices primarily solve?
options: Letting independent teams build and deploy services without waiting for each other
Making applications run twice as fast
